fields.name: Daily Life in Old Kingdom
fields.description: Explore how ordinary Egyptians lived, worked, and played during the Old Kingdom.
fields.history_text: The Old Kingdom (c. 2686–2181 BCE) was an era of remarkable stability and artistic achievement, often called the Age of Pyramids.

fields.name: Trade & Commerce
fields.description: Discover the trade networks that connected ancient Egypt to the wider world.

fields.name: Pharaohs & Power
fields.description: The hall of the kings — monuments, statues, and treasures of Egypt's most powerful rulers.

fields.name: Gods & Temples
fields.description: A journey through ancient Egyptian religion, mythology, and temple worship.

fields.name: Golden Mask of Tutankhamun
fields.description: The funerary mask of Tutankhamun is one of the masterpieces of Egyptian art. It is made of solid gold and weighs 10.23 kg.

fields.name: Khufu's Solar Boat
fields.description: A full-size vessel that was sealed into a pit in the Giza Pyramid complex, intended to carry the pharaoh in the afterlife.

fields.name: Statue of Ramses II
fields.description: A colossal granite statue of Ramses II, one of ancient Egypt's most celebrated pharaohs.

fields.name: Rosetta Stone (Replica)
fields.description: The key that unlocked the secrets of Egyptian hieroglyphics, bearing the same text in three scripts.

fields.name: Papyrus of Ani
fields.description: One of the finest examples of the Egyptian Book of the Dead, illustrating the journey to the afterlife.

fields.name: Hanging Obelisk Fragment
fields.description: A fragment of one of the great obelisks that once stood at the entrance to temple complexes.

fields.name: The Seated Scribe
fields.description: An immersive exhibition exploring the role of scribes in ancient Egyptian administration and culture.

fields.name: Royal Egyptian Jewelry
fields.description: Dazzling adornments worn by pharaohs and nobles, spanning three thousand years of craftsmanship.

fields.name: Tutankhamun's Throne
fields.description: The golden throne and its secrets — a detailed study of one of the most iconic royal artifacts.

fields.name: Egyptian Adult
fields.description: Standard entry for Egyptian adult visitors.

fields.name: Egyptian Student
fields.description: Discounted entry for Egyptian students with valid ID.

fields.name: Egyptian Child
fields.description: Entry for Egyptian children (under 12).

fields.name: Foreign Adult
fields.description: Standard entry for international adult visitors.

fields.name: Foreign Student
fields.description: Discounted entry for international students with valid ID.

fields.name: Foreign Child
fields.description: Entry for international children (under 12).

fields.name: GEM Discovery Challenge
fields.description: An interactive scavenger hunt for visitors of all ages.

fields.name: Arabic Audio Guide
fields.description: A self-guided audio tour narrating the museum's highlights in Arabic.

fields.name: English Audio Guide
fields.description: A self-guided audio tour narrating the museum's highlights in English.
A quick one-hour highlights tour perfect for the solo traveler on a tight schedule.
A full half-day deep dive into the museum's greatest treasures.
Curated for school groups, focusing on history and context.
